differential_abundance

Differential Abundance Analysis using ANCOMBC2

Loading in Packages

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(phyloseq)
library(ANCOMBC)
library(microbiome)
## 
## microbiome R package (microbiome.github.com)
##     
## 
## 
##  Copyright (C) 2011-2025 Leo Lahti, 
##     Sudarshan Shetty et al. <microbiome.github.io>
##     
##  
##     Note: we recommend switching to the SummarizedExperiment
##     framework, see microbiome.github.io/OMA and the mia package.
##     Minimum maintenance is provided microbiome R package but the
##     development efforts have switched to mia.
## 
## Attaching package: 'microbiome'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     alpha
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     transform
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

Wrangling steps

ps = readRDS('../processed_data/phyloseq_taxonomy.rds')
# setting seed
set.seed(412)


ps_genus = tax_glom(ps, 'Genus')

temp_relab = transform(ps_genus, 'compositional')
temp_filt = filter_taxa(temp_relab, function(x) mean(x) >= 0.0001, TRUE)

ps_filt = prune_taxa(taxa_names(temp_filt),ps_genus)
ps_filt
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 151 taxa and 115 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 115 samples by 20 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 151 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
## phy_tree()    Phylogenetic Tree: [ 151 tips and 150 internal nodes ]

HA Groups

Running ANCOMBC2 Analysis

There are three comparisons to be made for HA groups:

  • HA group 1 vs HA group 2

  • HA group 1 vs uninfected (negative)

  • HA group 2 vs uninfected (negative)

ANCOMBC2 on default takes the first alphabetical group in ha_group as the reference, meaning without modifications it will take HA group 1 as the reference. To compare HA group 2 vs uninfected, Frederick Huang (author of ANCOMBC2) suggested subsetting the data.

### HA Group 1 as reference ###
ha1_out = ancombc2(data = ps_filt, 
               fix_formula = 'ha_group', 
               p_adj_method = 'BH',
               prv_cut = 0.3) # increased due to small sample size
## Checking the input data type ...
## The input data is of type: phyloseq
## PASS
## Checking the sample metadata ...
## The specified variables in the formula: ha_group
## The available variables in the sample metadata: sample_identifier, num_bases, collection_date, country, state, county, precise_location, region, influenza_status, sex, influenza_level, ct_value, genotype, ha_subtype, na_subtype, HA_NA, ha_aggr, na_aggr, ha_group, na_group
## PASS
## Checking other arguments ...
## PASS
## Warning: The number of taxa used for estimating sample-specific biases is: 41
## A large number of taxa (>50) is required for the consistent estimation of biases
## Obtaining initial estimates ...
## Estimating sample-specific biases ...
## Loading required package: foreach
## 
## Attaching package: 'foreach'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     accumulate, when
## Loading required package: rngtools
## ANCOM-BC2 primary results ...
## Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ...
## For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis,
## they are marked in the 'passed_ss' column and will be treated as non-significant in the 'diff_robust' column.
## For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis, please refer to the package vignette.
ha1_statistical_table = ha1_out$res

### HA Group 2 as reference ###
# subset to only group2 and neg
# keep only group2 and neg samples
sub_ps_ha  <- ps_filt %>%
  subset_samples(ha_group %in% c("2", "neg"))

ha2_out = ancombc2(data = sub_ps_ha, 
               fix_formula = 'ha_group', 
               p_adj_method = 'BH',
               prv_cut = 0.3) # increased due to small sample size
## Checking the input data type ...
## The input data is of type: phyloseq
## PASS
## Checking the sample metadata ...
## The specified variables in the formula: ha_group
## The available variables in the sample metadata: sample_identifier, num_bases, collection_date, country, state, county, precise_location, region, influenza_status, sex, influenza_level, ct_value, genotype, ha_subtype, na_subtype, HA_NA, ha_aggr, na_aggr, ha_group, na_group
## PASS
## Checking other arguments ...
## PASS
## Warning: The number of taxa used for estimating sample-specific biases is: 45
## A large number of taxa (>50) is required for the consistent estimation of biases
## Obtaining initial estimates ...
## Estimating sample-specific biases ...
## ANCOM-BC2 primary results ...
## Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ...
## For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis,
## they are marked in the 'passed_ss' column and will be treated as non-significant in the 'diff_robust' column.
## For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis, please refer to the package vignette.
ha2_statistical_table = ha2_out$res

Volcano Plots

### HA Group1 vs HA Group2 ### 
ha1_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(ha1_statistical_table$q_ha_group2 < 0.05 & ha1_statistical_table$lfc_ha_group2 > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
ha1_statistical_table$association = ifelse(ha1_statistical_table$q_ha_group2 < 0.05 & ha1_statistical_table$lfc_ha_group2 < 0, "Depleted",ha1_statistical_table$association)

vplot_h1h2 <- ggplot(ha1_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_ha_group2), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_ha_group2)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot H1 vs H2") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_h1h2

### HA Group1 vs neg ### 
ha1_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(ha1_statistical_table$q_ha_groupneg < 0.05 & ha1_statistical_table$lfc_ha_groupneg > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
ha1_statistical_table$association = ifelse(ha1_statistical_table$q_ha_groupneg < 0.05 & ha1_statistical_table$lfc_ha_groupneg < 0, "Depleted",ha1_statistical_table$association)

vplot_h1neg <- ggplot(ha1_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_ha_groupneg), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_ha_groupneg)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot H1 vs neg") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_h1neg

### HA Group2 vs neg ###
ha2_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(ha2_statistical_table$q_ha_groupneg < 0.05 & ha2_statistical_table$lfc_ha_groupneg > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
ha2_statistical_table$association = ifelse(ha2_statistical_table$q_ha_groupneg < 0.05 & ha2_statistical_table$lfc_ha_groupneg < 0, "Depleted", ha2_statistical_table$association)

vplot_h2neg <- ggplot(ha2_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_ha_groupneg), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_ha_groupneg)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot H2 vs neg") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_h2neg

Log2Fold Change Plots

# extract Genus taxonomy from phyloseq for downstream plots
genus_tax <- data.frame(
  taxon = taxa_names(ps_filt),
  Genus = as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_filt))$Genus
)

### HA Group 1 vs HA Group 2 ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between H1 group and H2 group
h1h2_taxa_plot = ha1_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_ha_group2==T) 


# Plot the log 2 fold changes

h1h2_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(taxon,lfc_ha_group2)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

### HA Group 1 vs neg ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between H1 group and negative group
h1neg_taxa_plot = ha1_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_ha_groupneg==T) 

# Plot the log 2 fold changes

h1neg_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(taxon,lfc_ha_groupneg)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

### HA Group 2 vs neg ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between H2 group and negative group
h2neg_taxa_plot = ha2_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_ha_groupneg==T) 

# Plot the log 2 fold changes

h2neg_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(taxon,lfc_ha_groupneg)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

NA Groups

Running ANCOMBC2 Analysis

There are three comparisons to be made for HA groups:

  • NA group 1 vs NA group 2

  • NA group 1 vs uninfected (negative)

  • NA group 2 vs uninfected (negative)

### NA Group 1 as reference ###
na1_out = ancombc2(data = ps_filt, 
               fix_formula = 'na_group', 
               p_adj_method = 'BH',
               prv_cut = 0.3) # increased due to small sample size
## Checking the input data type ...
## The input data is of type: phyloseq
## PASS
## Checking the sample metadata ...
## The specified variables in the formula: na_group
## The available variables in the sample metadata: sample_identifier, num_bases, collection_date, country, state, county, precise_location, region, influenza_status, sex, influenza_level, ct_value, genotype, ha_subtype, na_subtype, HA_NA, ha_aggr, na_aggr, ha_group, na_group
## PASS
## Checking other arguments ...
## PASS
## Warning: The number of taxa used for estimating sample-specific biases is: 41
## A large number of taxa (>50) is required for the consistent estimation of biases
## Obtaining initial estimates ...
## Estimating sample-specific biases ...
## ANCOM-BC2 primary results ...
## Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ...
## For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis,
## they are marked in the 'passed_ss' column and will be treated as non-significant in the 'diff_robust' column.
## For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis, please refer to the package vignette.
na1_statistical_table = na1_out$res

### NA Group 2 as reference ###
sub_ps_na <- subset_samples(ps_filt, na_group %in% c("2", "neg")) # subseting data to remove ha group_1

na2_out = ancombc2(data = sub_ps_na, 
               fix_formula = 'na_group', 
               p_adj_method = 'BH',
               prv_cut = 0.3) # increased due to small sample size
## Checking the input data type ...
## The input data is of type: phyloseq
## PASS
## Checking the sample metadata ...
## The specified variables in the formula: na_group
## The available variables in the sample metadata: sample_identifier, num_bases, collection_date, country, state, county, precise_location, region, influenza_status, sex, influenza_level, ct_value, genotype, ha_subtype, na_subtype, HA_NA, ha_aggr, na_aggr, ha_group, na_group
## PASS
## Checking other arguments ...
## PASS
## Warning: The number of taxa used for estimating sample-specific biases is: 43
## A large number of taxa (>50) is required for the consistent estimation of biases
## Obtaining initial estimates ...
## Estimating sample-specific biases ...
## ANCOM-BC2 primary results ...
## Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ...
## For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis,
## they are marked in the 'passed_ss' column and will be treated as non-significant in the 'diff_robust' column.
## For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis, please refer to the package vignette.
na2_statistical_table = na2_out$res

Volcano Plots

### NA Group1 vs NA Group2 ### 
na1_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(na1_statistical_table$q_na_group2 < 0.05 & na1_statistical_table$lfc_na_group2 > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
na1_statistical_table$association = ifelse(na1_statistical_table$q_na_group2 < 0.05 & na1_statistical_table$lfc_na_group2 < 0, "Depleted",na1_statistical_table$association)

vplot_n1n2 <- ggplot(na1_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_na_group2), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_na_group2)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot N1 vs N2") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_n1n2

### NA Group1 vs neg ### 
na1_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(na1_statistical_table$q_na_groupneg < 0.05 & na1_statistical_table$lfc_na_groupneg > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
na1_statistical_table$association = ifelse(na1_statistical_table$q_na_groupneg < 0.05 & na1_statistical_table$lfc_na_groupneg < 0, "Depleted",na1_statistical_table$association)

vplot_n1neg <- ggplot(na1_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_na_groupneg), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_na_groupneg)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot N1 vs neg") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_n1neg

### NA Group2 vs neg ###
na2_statistical_table$association_ = ifelse(na2_statistical_table$q_na_groupneg < 0.05 & na2_statistical_table$lfc_na_groupneg > 0, "Enriched", "Not significant")
na2_statistical_table$association = ifelse(na2_statistical_table$q_na_groupneg < 0.05 & na2_statistical_table$lfc_na_groupneg < 0, "Depleted", na2_statistical_table$association)

vplot_h2neg <- ggplot(na2_statistical_table, aes(x = as.numeric(lfc_na_groupneg), y = -log10(as.numeric(q_na_groupneg)), color = association)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)",
       title = "Volcano Plot N2 vs neg") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  theme_bw()


vplot_h2neg

Log2Fold Change Plots

### NA Group 1 vs NA Group 2 ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between N1 group and N2 group
n1n2_taxa_plot = na1_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_na_group2==T) 

# Plot the log 2 fold changes

n1n2_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(taxon,lfc_na_group2)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

### NA Group 1 vs neg ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between N1 group and negative group
n1neg_taxa_plot = na1_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_na_groupneg==T) 

# Plot the log 2 fold changes

n1neg_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(taxon,lfc_na_groupneg)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

### NA Group 2 vs neg ### 
# Filter stats to only include taxa that are differentially abundant between N2 group and negative group
n2neg_taxa_plot = na2_statistical_table %>% 
  filter(diff_robust_na_groupneg==T) 

# Plot the log 2 fold change #

n2neg_taxa_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=taxon, y=lfc_na_groupneg)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()
